questions.question: Which hormone is responsible for the reabsorption of water in DCT and collecting duct? (Exam form 962)
questions.solution: By definition, ADH (Vasopressin) performs this role.

questions.question: Which step directly follows the administration of FSH-like hormones to the genetic mother in the MOET procedure?

options.A: Superovulation and subsequent mating with an elite bull (or AI)
options.B: Immediate surgical removal of the unfertilized eggs
options.C: Implantation of the eggs into the surrogate mother

questions.question: Which of the following is true concerning the production of human insulin in E. coli?

options.A: The A and B chains are produced separately in E. coli and then extracted and linked in vitro.
options.B: The complete proinsulin is synthesized and cleaved inside E. coli.
options.C: E. coli directly secretes fully formed, active insulin.
options.D: E. coli cannot produce human insulin proteins.

What is the primary function of the hormone thyroxine in the human body?
What is the term for the phenomenon where the presence of one hormone affects the production or function of another hormone?
What is the primary function of the hormone follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) in the human body?

options.B: To regulate the production of sperm
options.C: To regulate the production of eggs
options.D: To regulate the production of sex hormones

questions.question: What is the term for the phenomenon where the binding of a hormone to its receptor results in a decrease in the production of the hormone?
Thyroid hormones T3 and T4 require which mineral for their synthesis?
questions.solution: Thyroid hormones thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3) are iodine-containing hormones; iodine deficiency leads to hypothyroidism and goitre.
